name: phoneme_phon_demo
description: Phoneme-bag PHON lexicon. Train 3 nouns + 3 verbs with controlled phoneme overlap. Then probe held-out `riverrun` (untrained) whose phoneme bag is exactly river ∪ run (minus ɚ). Question: does the model assign riverrun bilateral routing because of its phonemic composition, without ever being explicitly trained or labeled as ambiguous?

probe_words.expected_composition: river phonemes (ɹ, ɪ, v) + run phonemes (ʌ, n); ɹ is shared between training nouns and verbs
probe_words.prediction: If phoneme-bag PHON works, riverrun gets bilateral NOUN_in and VERB_in (composed of inputs from N-trained and V-trained phoneme blocks). N/V ratio expected near 1, with slight noun-lean from the shared ɹ being supervised more toward NOUN (river + shore vs run alone).
